scope: Deriving per-change disposition, dependencies, reviewer routing, and status tree from the submitted payload (pure logic).

tests.name: nested add under add-create → latent (decision dep)
nested add under add-graft → latent
nested rename under add-create → latent (path-based, not target-based)
nested detach under add-graft → latent
nested add under top rename → queued (rename is existence dep only)
stacked renames → all queued (no add ancestors anywhere)
nested detach under no-op anchor → queued
Appendix A.1 dispositions match the worked example
top no-op is structural; every operative path includes it (observable via tree shape)
exactly one decision-dep ancestor → latent
two decision-dep ancestors → still latent (any add ancestor suffices)
deep no-op/rename chain (no add anywhere) → terminal change queued
sibling ops don't gate each other (an add sibling doesn't make a rename sibling latent)
no-op payload node is structural, carries no changeId
every queued change has exactly one entry; every latent change has zero
rename routes to the renamed taxon's owner, not the parent's
detach routes to the payload-parent's owner, not the child's
add-create routes to the payload-parent's owner
add-graft routes to the payload-parent's owner (not the grafted taxon's)
no-op produces no change in any queue
rename + nested add under same taxon route to different/same reviewers per §10.1
rename targeting C + nested add → both route to C's owner
graft preserves ownership: nested ops under graft route to grafted taxon's owner
proposer is not auto-reviewer of her own proposal
status view shape matches submitted payload (A.1)
every operative node carries a changeId; every no-op has none
status nodes echo op/id/name fields submitted (unknown keys dropped)
disposition is one of the five real states, or 'structural' for no-op
reason field is absent in Phase 5 (no rejection/invalidation paths exercised)
GET /proposals/{id} is readable by anyone (including null user)
GET /proposals/{id} unknown id → 404
GET /proposals/{id} malformed X-Username → 400
POST /proposals body's payload equals subsequent GET's payload
Appendix A.1 — multi-owner routing and a create cascade
derives four changes with the spec's initial dispositions
Appendix A.2 — cross-tree move, graft, and nested create
P1 detach is queued to Dana (owner of a2)
P2 graft is queued to Frank; nested create is latent and CONCRETE to Erin
decisionDeps
no add ancestors → empty decisionDeps
one add-create ancestor → that ancestor in decisionDeps
add-graft ancestor is also a decision dep
multiple add ancestors → top-down order
mixed ancestors: no-op + add → only add appears in decisionDeps
existenceDeps
rename includes ancestor existence deps + own implicit edge
detach includes own implicit edge
add-create has no own existence dep
add-graft has global-existence dep (parent: null)
ancestor existence deps record the live parent variant correctly
initial disposition
no add ancestors → queued
one add-create ancestor → latent
multiple add ancestors → still latent
deferred ⇒ latent invariant
every change with deferred reviewer has disposition latent
disposition does NOT consider existence deps
a change with non-trivial existence deps but no decision deps is queued
derive routing: concrete
rename routes to renamed taxon's owner
add-create routes to payload-parent's owner
add-graft routes to payload-parent's owner
detach routes to payload-parent's owner
concrete reviewer preserves registry casing
derive routing: deferred under add-create
add nested under add-create gets deferred reviewer
nested deferred refs point to IMMEDIATE create parent, not transitive
graft nested under add-create gets deferred reviewer
derive routing: nested under add-graft is concrete (not deferred)
add nested under add-graft routes concretely to graft target's owner
sequencer injection
derivation uses the injected mintChangeId verbatim
derivation purity
derive.ts does not import from store, handlers, or HTTP
derive: single-change emission
payload with only a single rename emits one change
only a no-op emits zero changes
no-op ancestor of an operative node emits zero changes for the no-op
add-create vs add-graft emit changes with distinct ops
single detach emits one change with op detach
each change carries proposalId and targetRootId
changeIds assigned in DFS pre-order
status tree per-node rules
no-op status node is structural with no changeId
queued rename status node has reviewer username
latent change under create has reviewer null
add-create status node has id:null and name set
add-graft status node has id and no name
detach status node has no children property
status tree shape mirrors payload
tree node count and nesting matches payload
status tree reason field
no status node has a reason field in Phase 5
no concrete-latent in current rules
no derived change is concrete-latent (deferred ⇒ latent, but latent ⇒ ? requires add ancestor)
